zh.lifeSummary: 當Sylvanis S Covey Sr於1818年2月3日出生在美國紐約州格林劍橋大學默里 巴爾的摩時，他的父親Reverend Walter Covey19歲，母親Mary Winn19歲。他於1836年12月18日與Nancy Zek結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他於1870年住在美國紐約州奧瑟格庫斯東。他去世於1911年2月24日在美國紐約州奧瑟格德卡度享年93歲，埋葬在美國紐約州奧瑟格伍斯特馬普爾 葛洛夫 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sylvanis S Covey Sr于1818年2月3日出生在美国纽约州格林劍橋大學默里 巴爾的摩时，他的父亲Reverend Walter Covey19岁，母亲Mary Winn19岁。他于1836年12月18日与Nancy Zek结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他于1870年住在美国纽约州奧瑟格庫斯東。他去世于1911年2月24日在美国纽约州奧瑟格德卡度享年93岁，埋葬在美国纽约州奧瑟格伍斯特馬普爾 葛洛夫 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Covey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish: shortened form of MacCovey, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Cobhthaigh (see Coffey ).

English: unexplained. Covey is used of ‘pantry’ in the early modern period and covey ‘little chap’ (a diminutive of cove, Middle English cof ‘bold, eager, courageous’; see Cove ) in the early nineteenth century, but there is no evidence to confirm either as the source for the surname.

variants.name: Coffey
meanings: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Cathbhadha ‘descendant of Cathbhadh’, a byname meaning ‘battle tent’, Ó Cathbhuadhaigh ‘descendant of Cathbhuadhach’ (‘battle victorious’), or Ó Cathmhogha ‘descendant of Cathmhugh’ (‘battle slave’).
shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Cobhthaigh ‘descendant of Cobhthach’, a byname meaning ‘victorious’.

historicEvents.title: Panic! of 1819
historicEvents.description: With the Aftermath of the Napoleonic Wars the global market for trade was down. During this time, America had its first financial crisis and it lasted for only two years.

historicEvents.title: Slavery Becomes Illegal in New York State
historicEvents.description: During the years 1799 to 1827, New York went through a period of gradual emancipation. A Gradual Emancipation Law was passed in 1799 which freed slave children born after July 4, 1799. However, they were indentured until 25 years old for women and 28 years old for men. A law passed 1817 which freed slaves born before 1799, yet delayed their emancipation for ten years. All remaining slaves were freed in New York State on July 4, 1827.
U.S. acquires vast tracts of Mexican territory in wake of Mexican War including California and New Mexico.
Abraham Lincoln issues Emancipation Proclamation, declaring slaves in Confederate states to be free.

historicEvents.title: Sorry Mr. President, You can't do that.
historicEvents.description: This Act was to restrict the power of the President removing certain office holders without approval of the Senate. It denies the President the power to remove any executive officer who had been appointed by the president with the advice and consent of the Senate, unless the Senate approved the removal during the next full session of Congress. The Amendment was later repealed.

historicEvents.title: Impeach the President!
historicEvents.description: Caused by many crimes and breaking the Tenure of Office Act, Many Senators and House Representatives became angry with President Johnson and began discussions of his Impeachment. After a special session of Congress, the Articles of Impeachment were approved by the House and then the Senate. Making Andrew Johnson the first President to be Impeached.

historicEvents.title: Plessy vs. Ferguson
historicEvents.description: A landmark decision of the U.S. Supreme Court upholding the constitutionality of racial segregation laws for public facilities if the segregated facilities were equal in quality. It's widely regarded as one of the worst decisions in U.S. Supreme Court history.

historicEvents.title: War with the Spanish
historicEvents.description: After the explosion of the USS Maine in the Havana Harbor in Cuba, the United States engaged the Spanish in war. The war was fought on two fronts, one in Cuba, which helped gain their independence, and in the Philippines, which helped the US gain another territory for a time.

en.lifeSummary: When Sylvanis S Covey Sr was born on 3 February 1818, in New Baltimore, Greene, New York, United States, his father, Reverend Walter Covey, was 19 and his mother, Mary Winn, was 19. He married Nancy Zek on 18 December 1836. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. He lived in Cooperstown, Otsego, New York, United States in 1870. He died on 24 February 1911, in Decatur, Otsego, New York, United States, at the age of 93, and was buried in Maple Grove Cemetery, Worcester, Otsego, New York, United States.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sylvanis S Covey Sr nasceu em 3 de fevereiro de 1818, em New Baltimore, Greene, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Reverend Walter Covey, tinha 19 anos e sua mãe, Mary Winn, tinha 19 anos. Ele casou-se com Nancy Zek em 18 de dezembro de 1836. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele viveu em Cooperstown, Otsego, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América em 1870. Ele faleceu em 24 de fevereiro de 1911, em Decatur, Otsego, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América, com 93 anos, e foi sepultado em Maple Grove Cemetery, Worcester, Otsego, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sylvanis S Covey Sr родился 3 февраля 1818 г. в местности ню балтимор, грин, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени Reverend Walter Covey было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Winn, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Nancy Zek 18 декабря 1836 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Он жил в местности купирстоун, оцего, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты в 1870 году. Он умер 24 февраля 1911 г. в местности декатур, оцего, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 93 лет и был похоронен в местности мапл гров кеметири, ворсестир, оцего, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты.
